visualGoal: This is a low-density how-to/explainer, not a product ranking. The visual job is to make ecosystem lock-in legible: show the four representative QR behaviors (Fanatec gated, MOZA medium, Simagic open, Simucube open) with real base photos, then let a comparison infographic and a PCD diagram carry the teaching load. Do NOT force product cards; the value is in the table, the decision tree, and the tip/forum callouts.

realProductPhotosNeeded.product: Cube Controls GT Pro (universal 70mm-PCD anti-lock-in rim)
realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: The article's poster child for buying the PCD/pattern instead of the brand. Currently TEXT + Amazon-search link only because /images/gear/racing/cube-gt.jpg is NOT in verified-product-images.json. Source a real photo before it can ever appear as a gearpick/shotgrid figure. Do not embed the existing unverified path.

realProductPhotosNeeded.product: Fanatec Podium Hub / Wheel Hub (QR2 FFB-enabling adapter for third-party rims)
realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: The single most-cited escape hatch in the article. A clean product shot of the hub would make the 'why doesn't my rim get FFB' section concrete. No verified image exists; text-only for now.

realProductPhotosNeeded.product: Simagic QR-A adapter (2025 lock-in break, 50/70mm PCD, built-in USB)
realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: Would visually anchor the '2025 cracked things open' claim. Accessory, no gear page. Text-only until sourced.

realProductPhotosNeeded.product: Logitech RS QR Adapter (opened G PRO to third-party rims, 70/50.8mm PCD)
realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: Optional. Reinforces the de-lock-in movement. Low priority; the brand table already carries the point in text.

editorialCompositePrompts.prompt: Extreme macro of a machined-aluminum sim-racing quick-release coupling half-clicked onto a direct-drive wheelbase shaft, warm tungsten rim light, shallow depth of field, cables softly out of focus, editorial 16:9, space on the left for a real wheelbase product overlay. No fake logos, no fake UI text.
editorialCompositePrompts.negativePrompt: fake logos, warped mechanism, impossible geometry, unreadable tiny text, procedural gradient art, plastic toy look

infographicPrompts.title: Quick-release lock-in by brand (2026)
content: Fanatec QR2 (6x70mm) - HIGHEST - FFB gated; needs Podium Hub for third-party FFB
MOZA QR (6x70mm) - MEDIUM - adapter is steering-only, buttons need USB
Simagic QR-A (50/70mm) - LOW - ~$89 adapter, built-in USB, opens Alpha Evo
Logitech RS QR (70/50.8mm) - MEDIUM/improving - ~$60 official adapter opened G PRO
Simucube SQR (70/50.8mm) - LOW - open by design, zero-lash
Asetek QR (pogo-pin) - LOW - power+data through QR, comparatively open
infographicPrompts.style: IgnitionSim command-center matrix, color-coded lock-in column (red->green), large readable text, real base photos as row markers for Codex overlay. This is the single highest-value visual for the article.

infographicPrompts.title: The 70mm / 50.8mm PCD loophole
content: PCD = bolt-hole spacing that clamps a rim to a QR
Most rims + real-car wheels use one of them
An adapter exposing 70mm/50.8mm is what crosses brands
infographicPrompts.style: Clean technical diagram of a bolt circle with 6x70mm pattern labeled, second smaller 50.8mm circle, minimal racing palette, large callouts. Teaches the one spec that decodes every spec sheet.

infographicPrompts.title: Decision tree: avoiding the quick-release trap
content: Bundle, never swap rims? QR barely matters
Want third-party rims later? Favor Simagic QR-A / Simucube SQR / Logitech RS QR
Set on Fanatec? Budget the Podium Hub
Already own third-party rims? Let the rims choose the base
infographicPrompts.style: Branching flowchart, IgnitionSim styling, large readable nodes, no product photos required.

curatorScenePrompts.prompt: Realistic adult Gen X sim-racing curator Duke Alvarez in a clean garage cockpit bay, holding a bare steering rim in one hand and gesturing at an empty wheelbase quick-release shaft, patient 'let me save you a mistake' energy, product overlay space beside him, practical not fantasy, 4:5 portrait. No fake logos.

openQuestions: Confirm current USD price of the Fanatec Podium Hub / Wheel Hub before any price is quoted (article intentionally leaves it unpriced).
Verify Logitech RS QR Adapter is still ~$60 and Simagic QR-A still ~$89 at cert time.

Should we source real photos of the four adapters (Podium Hub, QR-A, RS QR, MOZA QR) so the explainer can show the escape hatches, not just the bases? Highest-leverage adds are the qr-brand-matrix infographic + the Podium Hub photo.
Cube Controls GT Pro is text-only (no verified image). Source a real GT Pro photo if we want it as a body visual.
